quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 6 Quiz 6
quiz.subtitle: Refraction — Concepts
quiz.subject: Physics
quiz.topic: Grade 10 - Waves and Simple Harmonic Motion

questions.question: Refraction is the bending of a wave as it:
options: Bounces off the surface that it strikes
Passes from one medium to another at an angle
Spreads out after passing through a narrow opening
Combines with another wave during travel
questions.explanation: Refraction occurs when a wave changes direction as it crosses the boundary between two media at an angle.

questions.question: Refraction occurs because a wave changes its:
questions.explanation: The change in speed when entering a new medium causes the wave to bend. Frequency remains unchanged.

questions.question: When a wave passes from a faster medium into a slower medium, it bends:
questions.explanation: Slowing down causes the wave to bend toward the normal. Example: light entering glass from air.

questions.question: When a wave passes from a slower medium into a faster medium, it bends:
questions.explanation: Speeding up causes the wave to bend away from the normal. Example: light leaving glass into air.

questions.question: When a wave refracts from one medium to another, which quantity remains unchanged?
questions.explanation: Frequency is determined by the source and does not change when the wave enters a new medium. Speed and wavelength change.

questions.question: When light passes from air into glass (where it travels slower), its wavelength:
questions.explanation: $v = f\lambda$. With $f$ constant and $v$ decreasing, $\lambda$ must decrease.

questions.question: The refractive index of a medium is defined as the ratio of:
options: Speed of light in the medium to speed of light in a vacuum
Speed of light in a vacuum to speed of light in the medium
Frequency in the medium to frequency in a vacuum
Wavelength in the medium to wavelength in a vacuum
questions.explanation: $n = c/v$, where $c$ is the speed of light in a vacuum and $v$ is the speed in the medium.

questions.question: The refractive index of a vacuum is:
questions.explanation: By definition, $n = c/v$. In a vacuum, $v = c$, so $n = 1.0$. All other media have $n > 1$.

questions.question: A coin at the bottom of a swimming pool appears closer to the surface than it actually is. This is due to:
questions.explanation: Refraction bends light rays away from the normal as they leave the water, making the coin appear shallower — apparent depth.

questions.question: How many laws of refraction are there?
questions.explanation: There are two laws of refraction: (1) The incident ray, refracted ray, and the normal all lie in the same plane. (2) Snell's Law — the ratio of sin i to sin r is constant for a given pair of media.

questions.question: When a wave strikes a boundary at normal incidence (perpendicular to the surface), it:
questions.explanation: At normal incidence, the angle of incidence is 0°. The wave changes speed but does not change direction.

questions.question: Diamond has a higher refractive index than glass. This means light travels:
options: Faster in diamond than in glass
Slower in diamond than in glass
At the same speed in both
Faster in diamond only if the light is blue
questions.explanation: Higher $n$ means lower speed. $n = c/v$, so a larger $n$ means a smaller $v$. Light travels slower in diamond.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the concepts of refraction!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review when waves bend toward vs. away from the normal.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on what changes and what stays constant during refraction.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: definition of refraction, $n = c/v$, and the behavior of waves at boundaries.
